reviews.review_title: Skeptic Meets VR Magic: I recommend!
reviews.review_description:
I went into this app as a complete skeptic. I’m just a regular 9-to-5 chap, I don’t meditate, I don’t really know much about alternative states of consciousness, I’m just an ordinary fella is what I’m trying to say. So this is all new to me. I first tried this app using my partner’s headset, but after what I experienced, I had to buy a copy for myself, because I can’t deny that something genuinely unusual happened to me after using this app

To start off this VR experience pares everything back to the essentials; no flashy graphics or overwhelming effects, just light, sound, and space. That simplicity is part of its charm though, creating a quietly immersive environment that draws you in without demanding anything from you. It has a very friendly, intuitive interface, with a variety of “flavours” to choose from depending on your mood, whether you’re after calm, focus, or gentle stimulation. But don’t let its whimsical presentation fool you: behind the scenes, it’s serious work. The developer is highly active on Reddit and Discord and really knows their stuff. The app is backed by extensive research, and footnotes reference real scientific studies. The team behind it is well connected in the field of brain and consciousness research, so this isn’t just a creative experiment,it’s something grounded in evidence.

Each mode has its own personality, shifting from serene, meditative stillness to subtle, energizing rhythms (I found the strobing to be particularly effective. Though be warned if you have epilepsy or other conditions that are sensitive to this). It made the experience feel like a guided journey through different mental states rather than a conventional “VR experience” to check off a list.

What truly caught me off guard, though, was what followed for myself (I understand this is incredibly subjective). The night I first used this app I had the most vivid, lucid dream I’ve experienced since childhood. I found myself starting at home, effortlessly flying out of the window and gliding through my city. Objects bent and reshaped in my hands without breaking, as if reality itself was flexible. At one point I flew into my local cathedral, which had an impossible, dreamlike geometry inside that I can’t properly articulate, most likely inspired by the app itself. I didn’t want to wake up, I was having an incredible time, but the alarm dragged me back to reality and off to work.

I can’t explain it, and I’m not claiming miracles (it may be a coincidence) but the experience was undeniably uncanny. If nothing else, I hope I stumble into that level of lucidity again.

At its modest price, it’s absolutely worth giving it a try. I wholeheartedly recommend it. If you’re willing to keep an open mind and simply switch off for a while, you’ll almost certainly benefit from the effects this app offers. Who knows? You might even find yourself drifting into a lucid dream of your own.
I bought this app,tried it 2 times,and now it wont start anymore. I can not find to ask for a refund but this ie really bad.

developer_response.body: We would be happy to help you try and fix your problem or help you navigate the refund process, just email: braincandyapp@proton.me

reviews.review_title: Just what I’ve been looking for
reviews.review_description: I’ve wanted this ‘experience’ since I started in vr back in 2016.  A few apps have dabbled but this one fully leans into it and does it better than anyone else so far. The images fill your field of view and combined with the sound take you to another place.  There are a variety of different experiences and those can also be modded to an extent.  I bought the dlc already but I would like to see additional dlc.  There is one experience I wanted that isn’t here.  Many years ago I had an out of body experience and found myself moving through a swirling tunnel of light.  It would be cool if they could recreate that.
I had the most amazing experience last evening...there are so many different patterns.  i had a smoke before hand and this really made the experience even better!

reviews.review_title: Gift your self this candy,you deserve it
reviews.review_description:
A beautiful super detailed, thoughtful to the pixel, generous to the soul, wonderful to the eye, opening to the heart app, lovely example of guided art as a service to humans, if you wish to explore beauty, relaxation, Reconnection and inspiration.
This is made for you.

Just got it this morning so it's not a full review.

It's pretty slick except for what's possibly an audio glitch. Some audio tracks have crackles and pop. Is this intentional?
This app while paired with some legal Canadian medicinal products is a wonderful experience.  sit back in a zero gravity chair and let the visuals take over.

An original visual journey into the world of consciousness in VR. The experience is minimalist, but incredibly effective: you let yourself be lulled by the light and sound, perfect for unwinding after an intense session or stimulating your creativity. The different modes offer real variations in ambiance, between zen relaxation and energizing focus. Small reservation: it's not a game, so we forget about challenges or objectives. Visually, it remains sober, but it fits perfectly with the concept. At €6, it's a nice sensory surprise to try, almost a little mental reset with headphones. Ideal for anyone looking for an immersive break or a boost of inspiration without tiring their eyes... well, if you're not sensitive to blinking!

I was going through a rough patch in my life and I decided to get this game out of only hopes and dreams that it would treat my depression and adhd and it delivered I’m serious when I say this if I didn’t get this game I think I would have not been here on this earth for much longer on 1 to Saturn I rate this Neptune🥹

reviews.review_title: Closest thing to psychedelics out there
reviews.review_description:
Love this app.  Definitely the closest thing to a legal psychedelic experience available out there.  

Looks like the developer is also starting to add mindfulness exercises which is pretty cool - the concept has lots of great potential and it looks like the developer is frequently updating it and adding new content.  

Highly recommend 🍄🎨

WOW. This app feels like if Realms of Flow and Tripp had a baby—on steroids. It absolutely nails what both those apps were trying to achieve, but with way more depth and impact. Watching the promo video doesn’t even begin to capture the experience.

As a meditation app connoisseur who’s tried pretty much everything on the market, I can confidently say nothing touches this—especially if you're looking to relax, focus, recharge, or go full-on psychedelic. It’s seriously trippy. Once you’re inside, it feels like the kaleidoscope visuals are wrapping around you, and the sound moves in waves—completely immersive and beyond description.

There’s a huge variety of sessions, and each “brain candy” does exactly what it promises. At $9.99, it's a steal—especially while it's still in early access. Honestly, it could charge double and still be worth every penny.

This isn’t something you can describe—it’s something you have to feel. Major props to the developers… keep the Brain Candy coming, because I’ve definitely got a sweet tooth for this. 🍭🌀

This is the meditation app I have been searching for! I don’t wrote many reviews but this app needs to be known about! 

It is excellent and I wouldn’t change a thing. I have hundreds of Quest games and most every meditation app. This is the only app I use daily. 

The visuals are amazing. You can tell research went into the development. The controls are easy to use and intuitive. The sound and binaural rhythms sync perfectly with visuals. I am able to get into a trance state so easily. 

I hope the developer continues to add visuals and maybe even more meditation or breathing guides. As it is it’s well worth the money. 

If the developer reads this I’m curious if the quest would be powerfil to mimic a device like the neurovizr?this device is used with eyes closed and bright white lights flash to produce  within the mind

I love this app, it's so trippy and awesome, makes my brain tingle and it genuinely can be relaxing, but also intense (depending on the "candy" I choose). So that's pretty awesome. Totally worth the money!

Nice selection of intense effects! 
Curious what kind of ASMR will be integrated as indicated on the website.

Suggestion: It would be great if one could use the thumbsticks to interactively drive some parameters, e.g. the width/depth of the tunnels, the speed of texture movement, colors shift, sound timbre or alike. 

And if you are looking for other quality trippy and/or relaxing apps, be sure to also check out 'Realms of Flow' - it is very customizable.
Just a floating brain no information, tutorial, a white room and a floating brain, I can see my controllers only, no hands. So off for a refund, disappointed and frustrated!

developer_response.body: I'm sorry to hear you're having technical difficulties. This is an early access app and I am continuing to work out any bugs. I'm not sure what the reference to a white room is but you do need to be online the first time you run the app. The app presently does not have hands implemented, you do need to use your controllers. You should be able to click through to the main menu to see all the content. Please email info@braincandyapp.com if you'd like to give it another try.

reviews.review_description: What a unique experience! Visuals are crisp and clean with style variations for each set. This sure gives my brain some "candy" to chew on, sticky and sweet! Very interested in seeing/experiencing how this app continues to develop.
Nothing special

reviews.review_title: My brain likes it a lot
reviews.review_description: Very interesting experience, didnt expect that some of the things I saw there that I would consider super simple would have such an intense and interesting effect on my mind, very unique.

The visual variety is great & I especially appreciate the breathing module. 
I expect there will be a focus on additional modules and visual patterns; however I will need improvement to the control/ functionality for 5 stars on this app.

Listed in order of importance per my opinion:

1. Compatibility while head tracking is disabled. I noticed this game cannot launch in a dark room which feel like a huge issue for use prior to sleep. 

2. Especially pretaining to the breathing mode, and also applicable to other modes, sliders to manually adjust speed/ intensity. 

3. Some degree of audio setting  controls 

4. Hand control options to allow for selection and launching of a desired experience without the need to pick up a controller

Brain Candy is designed to induce neural responses to visual & audio stimuli rendered in VR.  The graphics make ample use of strobing light and you MUST avoid this title if you have medical conditions exacerbated by such effects.  Let's just cut through the cutesy B.S.--Brain Candy is purpose-designed to induce altered states of consciousness.  It uses science-based techniques to invoke a psychedelic/hypnotic state.  Brain Candy isn't a game or visualizer and I do not recommend it for casual users.  If you have experience with other VR apps employing such tools (e.g. SoundSelf, Visitations, Realms of Flow) you'll immediately recognize what Brain Candy is about.  Unlike those other therapeutic, meditative experiences Brain Candy uses brute-force to drag your brain into an altered state.  It isn't easy to review this app without extensively talking about pharmaceuticals, which should tell you something.  Brain Candy is exceptionally effective, but it might be too much for a casual audience just wanting to relax/chill.  If you're already a Psychonaut then Brain Candy tastes delicious.  Just be careful though because it is far more intense than a review can convey.

I have many apps and games, and Brain Candy provides yet a different experience than any other so far. Other apps have kaleidoscope type spinning mandalas and such and these are cool, no doubt. But Brain Candy has actual science behind it and not just for entertainment. The haptics and sounds are great. The trick IMO is to lock into the particular experience that resonates with you in the moment. Click on one of the bubbles and you might find that it doesn't resonate with you, so click on the next, and at some point, you are going to say, "Wow, that's it"! That's what your brain is craving at the moment. I locked into one this morning and stayed there for a very long time. It's like a body massage that you don't want to end, for your brain. I can't recommend highly enough for those searching for higher states of mind. To the Devs, can you make a "favorites" option so when you find something great, you can hop back in? Love it and keep going...Please!!

He probado todas las aplicaciones de este tipo, para mi esta es la mejor, no tiene comparacion, los sonidos binaurales y las imagenes hipnoticas en verdad te mueven tus ondas cerebrales, el nombre de Brain Candy hace honor a esta app

Brain Candy is like a massage for your brain. Some of the strobing effects are too strong for me, but most of them are a great way to unwind and just let your brain vibe on the visuals and sound. Highly recommend.
This is exactly what I've been waiting for and even better than I thought it could be
I think it's the best practice ever done for meditation and awareness studies.
Combined with hypnosis this takes my me time to a new level 🥰
And you’d be glad you’re not on three hits of strong a**d-!
This app tickles something deep, deep in my brain that no other meditation/visualization app has.
